task_completion.description: Verify that Fiji/ImageJ was opened and used to process the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Fiji/ImageJ main toolbar and image windows (e.g., showing specimen_micrograph.tif) should be visible in the screenshots.

task_completion.description: Verify the detailed particle measurements were saved to a CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'particle_measurements.csv' is visible in the '~/Fiji_Data/results/particles/' directory, or an open spreadsheet/Results table shows columns for Area, Perimeter, Circularity, Solidity, Feret's Diameter, and Aspect Ratio.

task_completion.description: Verify the size distribution histogram was generated and saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'size_distribution.png' is visible in the results directory, or an open image viewer shows a histogram/bar chart of particle areas.

task_completion.description: Verify the annotated micrograph with outlines and a scale bar was produced.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'annotated_micrograph.png' is visible or open on screen. The image must show outlines around the particles and include a visible scale bar (representing 10-50 µm).

task_completion.description: Verify the summary report text file was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'inclusion_report.txt' is visible in the '~/Fiji_Data/results/particles/' directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the summary report contains all required metrics and the QC result.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'inclusion_report.txt' are visible (e.g., in a text editor) and include: total particle count, mean area, area fraction, number of particles > 500 µm², and a clear 'PASS' or 'FAIL' QC result.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Fiji/ImageJ GUI to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show Fiji dialog boxes (like 'Analyze Particles', 'Set Scale', or 'Set Measurements') or the ImageJ Results table, rather than just terminal windows running headless scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the outputs appear to be genuinely generated by ImageJ.
integrity.visual_evidence: The histogram and annotated image should match typical ImageJ visual styles (e.g., standard ImageJ plot window aesthetics, standard ImageJ scale bar font/style, and typical particle outline overlays).

integrity.description: Verify the report contains actual calculated numerical data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'inclusion_report.txt' must contain specific, plausible numerical values for the metrics (e.g., a mean area around 13.7 µm²), not just placeholder text, empty fields, or copied prompt instructions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input image is 1000x2000 pixels, 8-bit grayscale. The correct calibration is 0.1725 µm/pixel. The minimum particle size filter is 5 µm². The critical threshold is 500 µm² with a QC limit of 5 particles. When correctly calibrated and filtered, the mean area of the particles should be approximately 13.70 µm².
